_comment: OS-FOUNDATIONAL capabilities — the set every project inherits automatically via the upgrade path (os-inherit.mjs). This is the 'In OS -> OS Upgrade Path -> Every Project Inherits' link of the capability chain (capabilities/CAPABILITY-LEDGER.md). A capability is NOT complete until it is listed here and a project's `os-inherit sync` brings it in + `os-inherit check` keeps it byte-current. Agents propagate via os-sync (base+overlay); tools/contracts/skills propagate via os-inherit (vendored, drift-checked) so a project is self-contained (works in CI without the OS mounted).
enginesNote: ENGINES are OS-OWNED runtime capability packs vendored as a WHOLE DIRECTORY (byte-identical) into the consuming app, with a per-engine INHERITED-<key>.json sha-record. They are the LOCK that makes Delivery OS the platform/runtime and the app a mere INSTALLER: `os-inherit sync` installs the engine; `os-inherit engine-check` is the fail-closed drift gate (installed-vs-recorded AND recorded-vs-canonical) plus a DDL PARITY check. ddlParity declares the canonical engine migration set (source of truth for the SHAPE) and the app's APPLIED instance migrations (renumbered/split per the app's sequence) — the check asserts they are STRUCTURALLY equivalent (same tables/columns/CHECK bodies/indexes/triggers/functions/policies/RLS), so the engine OWNS the DDL and divergence is caught. The app applies an equivalent instance; it does NOT re-run the canonical files.

ddlParity.appMigrationsNote: The app's APPLIED engine-migration FILE PATHS are PER-APP / PROJECT-LOCAL — they are NOT declared here (that would be single-tenant). Each installer declares its own applied paths in its project-local .claude/os/engine.config.json: {"workflow-engine":{"appMigrations":["migrations/…"]}}. `os-inherit engine-check` reads THAT per-app declaration for the DDL-parity inputs (falling back to a shared-manifest ddlParity.appMigrations only if no project-local config exists — backward compat). The shared manifest still owns the CANONICAL set (canonicalDir) — the source of truth for the SHAPE. Admin declares its 0034/0037/0038/0039/0041; the demo-app declares its verbatim 0001-0004; PLOS will declare its own renumbered set on install.
ddlParity.note: Structural equivalence is asserted between the canonical engine migration set (canonicalDir) and the app's APPLIED instance (declared per-app in engine.config.json). Reference mappings: Canonical 0001_engine_core.sql == Admin 0034 + 0038 (0034 creates workflow_step with a 3-value effect CHECK; 0038 widens it via DROP+ADD CONSTRAINT to the canonical 4-value set incl. await-callback — final state equivalent). Canonical 0002_engine_await_loop.sql == Admin 0037. Canonical 0003_engine_agent_runner.sql == Admin 0039. Canonical 0004_engine_agent_id.sql == Admin 0041. The demo-app applies all four canonical files VERBATIM (byte-identical) into its own sequence. The app's outbox COMMENT ON COLUMN / outbox table is installer-owned infra, NOT engine DDL — canonical 0001 deliberately omits the outbox; the structural check ignores non-engine objects.

agentsNote: agents propagate via os-sync (base+overlay merge), not os-inherit; listed here for a complete view of the OS foundation.
